title: Asia Pacific: China+1 is being shaped by China itself
gist: Despite the China+1 diversification narrative, China has grown its share of global manufacturing value added by over 6 percentage points since 2017 to more than one-third of world output, and its push into high-tech and green manufacturing is expected to consolidate that lead. Relocation to Vietnam and India has been selective and confined to lower-value sectors like electronics assembly and textiles.

insights.claim: China has increased its share of global manufacturing value added by over 6 percentage points since 2017 and now accounts for more than one-third of all manufactured goods worldwide.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: The China+1 diversification thesis is overstated at the aggregate level; investors betting on wholesale China manufacturing decline are fighting the actual trend.
insights.quote: China has increased its share of global manufacturing value added by over 6 percentage points since 2017, now accounting for more than one-third of all manufactured goods worldwide.
insights.locator: China continues to shape the global manufacturing landscape

insights.claim: China continues to shape the global manufacturing landscape even as some production shifts to other emerging Asian markets, meaning diversification is happening around China rather than at its expense.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Position for a China-centric Asian supply chain, not a China-replacement one; 'China+1' beneficiaries remain dependent on Chinese inputs.
insights.locator: China continues to shape the global manufacturing landscape

insights.claim: Vietnam and India have made notable gains in specific sectors such as electronics and textiles, but the overall relocation of production out of China has been selective rather than broad-based.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Exposure to Vietnam and India supply-chain plays should be scoped to specific sectors (electronics assembly, textiles), not treated as a general China substitute.
insights.quote: While countries like Vietnam and India have made notable gains in specific sectors, such as electronics and textiles, the overall relocation of production from China has been selective.
insights.locator: While countries like Vietnam and India have made notable gains

insights.claim: China retains a dominant role in higher-value segments and complex goods, so reliance on Chinese supply chains remains a significant factor even where assembly has moved abroad.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Upstream/complex-component exposure to China is sticky; firms claiming China exit may still carry deep Chinese input dependency as a hidden risk.
insights.quote: The ongoing reliance on Chinese supply chains remains a significant factor, with China maintaining a dominant role in higher-value segments and complex goods.
insights.locator: The ongoing reliance on Chinese supply chains

themes.theme: Industrial upgrading and the opening for others

insights.claim: China's industrial strategy, focused on high-tech and green manufacturing, is expected to consolidate its manufacturing lead over the short to medium term.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Watch Chinese high-tech and green-manufacturing capacity as a structural competitive threat in those verticals rather than expecting share loss.
insights.quote: China's industrial strategy, focusing on high-tech and green manufacturing, is expected to consolidate its lead in the short to medium term.

insights.claim: Other emerging Asian nations can find opportunities by complementing China's industrial upgrading, particularly in electronics and battery supply chains.
insights.speaker: Keith Mason
insights.so_what: Screen emerging-Asia investments for complementary roles in electronics and battery supply chains rather than head-on competition with China.
insights.quote: Other emerging Asian nations can find opportunities by complementing China's industrial upgrading, particularly in electronics and battery supply chains.
insights.locator: Other emerging Asian nations can find opportunities
